claim_a: Human vigilance with automated systems drops 20-50% after 30 minutes [E] [S15] (from SQ-1)
claim_b: Human performance with automation degrades 20-50% after 30 minutes | [S15] | E | A | High (from SQ-3)
why_they_differ: SQ-1 presents this as potentially relevant to PKM tool usage patterns, while SQ-3 uses it as evidence against knowledge compounding, suggesting performance decline rather than growth.
impact: Creates ambiguity about whether automation degradation applies to PKM systems and whether it supports or contradicts knowledge compounding theories

claim_a: Without retention data, any TAM calculation for AI agents targeting PKM users is building on quicksand (from SQ-1)
claim_b: The evidence suggests a bifurcated market: high-stakes domains demonstrate clear technical need for calibration... mass-market users appear satisfied with confident-but-miscalibrated responses (from SQ-5)
why_they_differ: SQ-1 claims no market segmentation data exists, while SQ-5 confidently describes market bifurcation between high-stakes and mass-market segments.
which_is_stronger: A - SQ-1 correctly identifies lack of PKM-specific retention and segmentation data, while SQ-5's market bifurcation is inferred from technical capabilities rather than actual user behavior data
impact: Undermines confidence in market segmentation assumptions that might drive product positioning and pricing strategies
